title: quercus robur subsp. astrotricha
intro: Quercus robur subsp. astrotricha, also known as the English oak, is a subspecies of the common oak tree, characterized by its distinctive hairy leaves and robust growth habit. The medicinal properties of Quercus robur subsp. astrotricha lie in its high tannin content, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant, making it a valuable herbal remedy for cardiovascular health and digestive issues. Quercus robur subsp. astrotricha can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, allowing for various forms of herbal supplementation.
